title: Name/secure formation reviewer role (blocking gate role)
context: The provenance and pedagogical review gates require a named human reviewer to sign off on formation accuracy before any glyph set ships. For Latin, this is a maintainer-level role requiring literacy/formation expertise; for non-Latin scripts (M2+), a script-literate reviewer who is a native/fluent writer or qualified teacher of that script is mandatory. Without a named reviewer, the pilot-007 5-glyph end-to-end loop cannot close, and M0 cannot exit.
objective: Identify and confirm at least one named formation reviewer for Latin (and document the role requirements for future non-Latin script reviewers), producing a committed reviewer-roles document and at least one named Latin reviewer willing to sign the per-glyph checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: A reviewer-roles document is committed defining the qualifications for: (a) Latin/technical formation reviewer and (b) script-literate reviewer for non-Latin scripts.
At least one named Latin formation reviewer is confirmed and recorded (name/handle + contact + affiliation if applicable + conflict-of-interest declaration).
The per-glyph review checklist template is drafted and attached (stroke order, direction, guideline alignment, start dots, accuracy vs. cited source).

Reviewer-recruitment guidance for non-Latin scripts is outlined (where to find script-literate reviewers: language-community organizations, SIL-style literacy programs, university linguistics departments).
resources: PLAN.md section 8 (Quality, review & risk gates), section 11 (Governance, roles)
PLAN.md section 2 (beneficiaries — language community orgs, SIL literacy programs)
TASKS.md M0 acceptance criteria for pilot-007
output: A committed reviewer-roles document (e.g. docs/reviewer-roles.md) with the per-glyph checklist template, plus a recorded confirmation of the named Latin formation reviewer.
